name: Oliete and the Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal Loop
name_original: Oliete - La Portadilla – circular por la Ermita de la Virgen del Cantal
description: Hike the moderate 3.4-mile Oliete - La Portadilla circular trail, visiting the Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal and La Portillada gorge.
region_intro: Hiking around Oliete, a village in Teruel, Spain, offers a diverse landscape for outdoor exploration. The region is characterized by unique natural formations, including a large cavern known as Sima de San Pedro, and the Martin River with its Dam of Cueva Foradada. Expansive olive groves define much of the terrain, alongside areas featuring historical Iberian ruins. This blend of natural and cultural elements creates varied hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Oliete The most popular hiking route is Hermitage Virgin Sierra de Arcos from Ariño (GR 262), a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route follows a section of the GR 262 long-distance path. Another top favourite among local hikers is Oliete and the Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal Loop, a moderate 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This circular route explores the area around Oliete, including a local hermitage. Local hikers also love the Mirador de la Silleta loop from PR-TE 119, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a viewpoint, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Hiking around Oliete is defined by ancient olive groves, river valleys, and historical sites, including Iberian ruins. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Oliete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Oliete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Oliete?
A: Oliete offers several hiking trails, with around 8 routes currently available on komoot. These range from easy walks to moderate excursions, providing options for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Oliete?
A: Hiking in Oliete reveals a diverse landscape characterized by ancient olive groves, river valleys, and unique natural formations. You'll encounter the impressive Sima de San Pedro cavern, the tranquil Martin River with its Dam of Cueva Foradada, and areas rich in historical Iberian ruins. The region is known for its 'whimsical eccentricities shaped by nature,' offering varied and interesting terrain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Oliete?
A: Yes, Oliete has several easy trails suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Silleta loop from PR-TE 119&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.7 km trail that leads to a viewpoint, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Engravings – Los Chaparros Rock Shelter loop from Casas de los Bataneros&lt;/a&gt; is another easy option at 5.1 km, offering a glimpse into local history.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see on the trails?
A: The region is home to several significant natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the massive Sima de San Pedro, a natural cavern, or visit the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3618839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Foradada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; on the Martin River. For history enthusiasts, routes often pass by ancient Iberian ruins like El Palomar and El Cabezo of San Pedro, recognized as sites of 'Cultural Interest'.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Oliete?
A: Yes, Oliete features several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979637651/oliete-and-the-hermitage-of-the-virgin-of-cantal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oliete and the Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.4 km path exploring the area around the village and a local hermitage. Another option is the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Silleta loop from PR-TE 119&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Oliete?
A: Oliete offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the olive groves and historical sites. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round outdoor activity.

Q: Is there a long-distance trail that passes through Oliete?
A: Yes, a section of the GR 262 long-distance path runs through the area. You can experience a part of it on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979745327/hermitage-virgin-sierra-de-arcos-from-arino-gr-262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage Virgin Sierra de Arcos from Ariño (GR 262)&lt;/a&gt; route, which is a moderate 12.7 km trail.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Oliete?
A: The hiking routes in Oliete are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 8 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical intrigue, and the tranquil experience of walking through ancient olive groves and along river valleys.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Yes, many trails offer scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Silleta loop from PR-TE 119&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to lead you to a viewpoint. Additionally, the Sima de San Pedro is renowned for its breathtaking sunsets, offering a spectacular natural vista.

Q: Can I find places to picnic or relax by the water in Oliete?
A: Absolutely. The Dam of Cueva Foradada, situated on the Martin River, provides a picturesque setting ideal for leisurely afternoons and picnics. It's a tranquil spot to relax by the water after your hike.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the famous olive groves of Oliete?
A: Many of the trails in Oliete weave through the expansive olive groves that define the region's landscape. Walking through these ancient, revitalized groves is a unique and pleasant hiking experience, connecting you with the area's agricultural heritage.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of hikes in Oliete?
A: The trails around Oliete offer a mix of difficulty levels. You'll find easy walks perfect for a relaxed outing, as well as moderate excursions that provide a bit more challenge. There are no difficult-rated trails listed, making the area accessible for most hikers.
tips: Just a couple of kilometers from the center of Oliete is this picturesque spot surrounded by a typical Teruel landscape. You can take the path to make a circular route passing by the Hermitage of the Virgen del Cantal.

Outside the urban area, surrounded by hundred-year-old pine and cypress trees, there is a large and ancient center of devotion: the Virgen del Cantal hermitage. It was built in brick (dome and belfry) and masonry. It preserves the image of the Virgen del Cantal, belonging to the so-called popular art, always difficult to date. 17th century (1660)

faqs.question: What is the estimated hiking time for the Oliete - La Portadilla circular route?
faqs.answer: The estimated hiking time for this circular route is approximately 1 hour and 38 minutes. This allows you to comfortably enjoy the scenery and points of interest along the way.

faqs.question: Is the Oliete - La Portadilla trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as a 'moderate hike,' making it suitable for most fitness levels. While it features mostly accessible paths, sure-footedness is recommended due to some varied terrain. It's a good option for those with some hiking experience.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Oliete - La Portadilla circular route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. You'll encounter the rural landscapes of Bajo Aragon and the picturesque La Portillada. A significant highlight is the Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal, an ancient center of devotion surrounded by centuries-old pine and cypress trees.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by the rural landscapes of Bajo Aragon, Teruel. It features mostly accessible paths, but as a moderate hike, you should expect some varied ground. The route includes an elevation gain of about 190 meters and a similar descent, so there will be some uphill and downhill sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Oliete - La Portadilla circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking in Aragon. These seasons also showcase the natural beauty of the olive groves and rural landscapes at their best. Summer can be hot, so an early start is advisable if hiking then.

faqs.question: Is the Oliete - La Portadilla trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through rural areas, near livestock, or around the cultural sites like the Hermitage, to ensure safety and respect for the local environment.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Oliete - La Portadilla trail?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending in Oliete, you can typically find parking within the town itself. Specific parking areas for hikers are usually signposted or available near the main access points to the trails. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to hike the Oliete - La Portadilla circular trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is the Oliete - La Portadilla circular route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and manageable length of 5.44 kilometers, this circular route can be suitable for families with children who are accustomed to hiking. The cultural interest of the Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal and the natural scenery offer engaging points for all ages.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sendero del Río Martín, Por el río Martín, Ruta Íbera, and camino de la margen izquierda, offering connections to other local hiking networks.

faqs.question: What is unique about the Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal?
faqs.answer: The Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal is a significant cultural and spiritual landmark. It's a brick and masonry structure housing a 17th-century image of the Virgen del Cantal, reflecting popular art. Its tranquil setting, surrounded by centuries-old pine and cypress trees, makes it a peaceful and historically rich stop on the trail.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal
